name: smoke
description: A sanity check tool to verify tool-calling is working. Triggered when the user asks what day it is.

query.description: The user's question about the date or time

name: github/create_issue
description: Files a new issue in a GitHub repository.

name: github/search_issues
description: Search for GitHub issues in the repository.

name: github/comment_issue
description: Adds a comment to an existing GitHub issue.

issue_number.description: The issue number to comment on

name: fs/write
description: Write content to a file. The path must be relative to the project root, e.g. src/main.ts or README.md. Never use absolute paths or placeholder paths like /path/to/file.

path.description: Relative path from the project root, e.g. src/main.ts or README.md

content.description: The full content to write to the file

name: fs/read
description: Read the content of a file. The path must be relative to the project root, e.g. src/main.ts or README.md.

path.description: Relative path from the project root, e.g. src/main.ts

name: fs/bash
description: Run a shell command in the project root directory.

command.description: The shell command to run, e.g. npm test or git status

name: fs/glob
description: List files in the project matching a pattern, e.g. *.ts or *.json.

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match, e.g. *.ts or src/**/*.ts
